channel.title: Frontion News - Tier 1 Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Pope Leo XIV warns of 'profound crisis' in historic address to Spanish parliament
item.description: In his first speech to a national parliament, Pope Leo XIV called for respect for migrants and international law, warning that escalating conflicts and polarization have pushed the world into a deep crisis.

item.title: China's Xi vows unwavering support for North Korea in rare Pyongyang visit
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping made a rare visit to North Korea, pledging to safeguard common interests and fight 'hegemony' alongside Kim Jong Un, signaling deepening ties amid regional tensions.

item.title: Magnitude 7.8 earthquake kills at least 35 in southern Philippines
item.description: A powerful 7.8-magnitude earthquake struck off Mindanao island, killing at least 35 people and injuring dozens. Buildings collapsed in General Santos City as authorities launched search and rescue operations.

item.title: Apple unveils AI-powered Siri overhaul at WWDC 2026
item.description: Apple announced a major revamp of its voice assistant at its annual developers conference, introducing 'Siri AI' with better voice recognition and a standalone app, marking the company's long-awaited push into generative AI.

item.title: WHO urges Uganda to reconsider Congo border closure over Ebola outbreak
item.description: The WHO chief called on Uganda to reconsider closing its border with DR Congo, where confirmed Ebola cases have risen to 515 with over 100 deaths. Armed groups threaten response efforts.

item.title: Lebanon says Israel has bombed it nearly 3,500 times during ceasefire
item.description: Israel has carried out nearly 3,500 air strikes on Lebanon since the April ceasefire, Lebanon's defense minister said, highlighting ongoing violations despite the truce agreement.

item.title: Taiwan calls Chinese Coast Guard patrols to its east provocative
item.description: Chinese Coast Guard patrols to the east of Taiwan are a "provocative act" and the military will closely coordinate with the island's Coast Guard in responding, Taiwan's defense minister said.

item.title: Chinese military maneuvered near Taiwan as global tech executives gathered
item.description: As AI heavyweights including Nvidia, Intel and SK Group championed Taiwan's significance for the global supply chain, a hostile exchange with China was brewing at sea, with Chinese military activity intensifying near the island.

item.title: South Korea to unveil major investment project as AI boom swells tax revenue
item.description: South Korea will soon unveil a large-scale investment project, President Lee Jae Myung said, as the government looks to use buoyant tax revenues from the chip sector amid the AI boom.

item.title: Gulf stock markets drop as Middle East tensions weigh on investors
item.description: Major Gulf stock markets fell as renewed Middle East tensions between Iran and Israel weighed on investor sentiment, despite signals that both sides had halted strikes.

item.title: Bennett slams Netanyahu for "normalizing" Iran attacks
item.description: Former Israeli PM Naftali Bennett criticized Netanyahu for what he called accepting periodic Iranian attacks as normal, amid renewed missile exchanges between Israel and Iran.

item.title: Knesset passes law deducting terror victim compensation from PA tax revenues
item.description: Israel's parliament passed legislation allowing compensation for terror victims to be deducted from Palestinian Authority tax revenues, countering payments to families of attackers.

item.title: Franco-German FCAS fighter jet program reportedly cancelled
item.description: Reports indicate the Future Combat Air System (FCAS), Europe's planned next-generation fighter jet program involving France, Germany and Spain, has been cancelled amid disagreements.

item.title: Ukraine jails former Supreme Court chief justice for graft
item.description: Ukraine's former Supreme Court chief justice was sentenced to prison on corruption charges, part of Kyiv's anti-corruption crackdown amid the ongoing war with Russia.

item.title: IAEA urges Iran to re-engage on nuclear inspections amid Western pressure
item.description: UN nuclear watchdog chief Rafael Grossi called on Iran to "re-engage" so inspections can resume at sites the US and Israel bombed, as the US led a push for a resolution at the agency's board.

item.title: Israel defies Trump with Iran strikes to gain peace table influence
item.description: In launching renewed strikes on Iran in apparent defiance of Donald Trump, Israel has tried to secure a say at the peace negotiating table, where it has been kept at arm's length by the US president.

item.title: Ukrainian drone strike kills one in Russian-annexed Crimea
item.description: A Ukrainian drone struck a train in Crimea, killing one person and injuring another, the Russian-installed governor said, as Ukraine continued strikes on Russian-held territory.

item.title: South Korea KOSPI crashes over 8% as Fed fears spark tech rout
item.description: South Korea's stock benchmark plunged over 8%, tripping circuit breakers, after robust US jobs data lifted bets on a Federal Reserve rate hike and unleashed a selloff in tech-heavy markets.

item.title: UN says children dying in Cuba due to strict US sanctions
item.description: A UN report highlighted that children are dying in Cuba due to strict US sanctions that limit access to medical supplies and essential goods.

item.title: Trump administration seeks to revoke US citizenship of 17 immigrants
item.description: The Trump administration is pursuing denaturalization proceedings against 17 naturalized US citizens accused of fraud, crimes, or immigration violations, marking an unprecedented expansion of citizenship revocation efforts.

item.title: Federal judge rules Trump's $100,000 H-1B visa fee unlawful
item.description: A federal judge struck down the Trump administration's $100,000 fee on new H-1B visas for skilled foreign workers, ruling it an unlawful tax that Congress never authorized.

item.title: EU sanctions IRGC Navy unit over Strait of Hormuz blockade
item.description: The European Union imposed sanctions on Iran's IRGC Navy unit in response to the continued blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, which has disrupted global oil shipments.

item.title: French fighter jet shoots down drone in Latvian airspace during NATO mission
item.description: A French fighter jet on a NATO air policing mission shot down a drone that entered Latvian airspace from Russia, the first such interception since the Ukraine war began.

item.title: Houthis ban Israeli shipping from Red Sea amid Iran-Israel conflict
item.description: Yemen's Iran-backed Houthis renewed threats against Israeli shipping in the Red Sea, warning of continued attacks as the conflict between Iran and Israel escalates.

item.title: Ukraine foils Russian plot to assassinate senior military intelligence official
item.description: Ukrainian authorities said they foiled a Russian plot to assassinate a senior military intelligence official, detaining suspects linked to Russian intelligence services.
